input.initialPrompt: Before creating anything, show me exactly what files would be generated if I create a Blazor Web App called MyBlazorApp in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. Don't actually create the project yet.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

data.reasoningText: The user wants to see what files would be generated by creating a Blazor Web App with interactive server rendering, without actually creating the project. I can use `dotnet new blazor` with the `--dry-run` flag to show what would be generated.

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run 2>&1", "description": "Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity", "initial_wait": 15}

data.content:
Here's the dry-run output. The template would generate **68 files** across these groups:

| Directory | Contents |
|---|---|
| `Components/` | `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor` |
| `Components/Layout/` | `MainLayout`, `NavMenu`, `ReconnectModal` (`.razor`, `.razor.css`, plus `.razor.js` for reconnect) |
| `Components/Pages/` | `Counter`, `Error`, `Home`, `NotFound`, `Weather` (all `.razor`) |
| Root | `Program.cs`, `MyBlazorApp.csproj`, `appsettings.json`, `appsettings.Development.json` |
| `Properties/` | `launchSettings.json` |
| `wwwroot/` | `app.css`, `favicon.png` |
| `wwwroot/lib/bootstrap/` | Full Bootstrap dist (CSS + JS, ~50 files) |

A NuGet restore would also run automatically after creation. Say the word when you'd like me to actually create it.
